best_for: The default for most sites: one domain with a clean SEO signal per locale and the simplest hosting and deploy story
avoid_when: Truly separate regional businesses needing separate branding or infrastructure per market
tradeoffs: Cheapest to run and easiest to reason about; every route needs locale-awareness baked in from the start

best_for: Larger orgs wanting per-region infrastructure or CDN routing flexibility while keeping one brand domain
avoid_when: Small teams where this adds DNS cert and deploy overhead for no real benefit
tradeoffs: Independent scaling and caching per region; more infrastructure to provision and keep in sync

best_for: Genuinely separate regional entities with different legal entity pricing or catalog needing the strongest local-market trust signal
avoid_when: A single global product with shared content since ccTLDs fragment SEO authority across domains
tradeoffs: Highest local trust and clearest regional signal to search engines; the most expensive and slowest to operate

best_for: Any product shipping partial translations, defining an explicit fallback order such as tr to en to default so missing keys degrade to a real language never a raw key
avoid_when: Products requiring full translation coverage before any locale ships which is rare and usually not worth the delay
tradeoffs: Ships new locales incrementally without breaking the UI; requires discipline to eventually close fallback gaps rather than leave them permanent

best_for: Marketing pages where imagery carries cultural or legal meaning such as people gestures text-in-image or currency symbols swapped per locale not just text
avoid_when: Purely functional UI icons with no cultural content
tradeoffs: Avoids tone-deaf or legally risky imagery per market; multiplies asset production and CMS complexity

option: Detect via Accept-Language then let the user override and persist the choice
best_for: First visit for any product without forced regional routing that should respect browser preference without trapping the user in it
tradeoffs: Good default UX; skipping the override-and-persist step reopens the redirect trap this pattern exists to avoid

option: hreflang tags generated from the same locale source of truth
best_for: Every localized route needs hreflang alternates generated programmatically from the routing config rather than hand-maintained per page
avoid_when: A single-locale site with nothing to alternate against
tradeoffs: Prevents the hreflang errors that are among the most common international SEO mistakes; one generation function instead of many hand-written tag sets

option: RTL support via logical CSS properties
best_for: Any product shipping Arabic Hebrew or Farsi using margin-inline and padding-inline instead of margin-left and margin-right with dir set per locale
avoid_when: Locale sets with no RTL languages where the extra testing surface is not yet needed
tradeoffs: Logical properties make RTL close to free once adopted; retrofitting RTL onto a hardcoded left-right codebase later is expensive

option: ICU plural rules via the chosen library
best_for: Any language beyond English in the locale set since plural rules vary by language and cannot be hardcoded as a two-way conditional
avoid_when: English-only products where the naive two-category assumption already happens to hold
tradeoffs: Correct grammar across all target languages; requires messages authored with plural categories rather than string concatenation

option: No string concatenation for translated sentences
best_for: Every translated sentence built via a single message with placeholders through ICU MessageFormat or an equivalent rather than joining translated fragments
tradeoffs: Word order and grammar vary by language so concatenation breaks correctness in most non-English languages not just as an edge case

option: Legal and compliance text localized with the same rigor as marketing copy
best_for: Terms of service privacy policy pricing and tax disclosures and cookie consent text translated and reviewed with an equal or higher bar than product copy
avoid_when: Marketing-only translation effort that treats legal pages as low priority
tradeoffs: Untranslated or poorly translated legal text is both a trust failure and a real compliance gap in some jurisdictions

option: Locale-aware currency and tax display kept separate from the payment-processing currency
best_for: E-commerce or SaaS pricing pages where display currency follows the user's locale via Intl.NumberFormat while the actual charged currency follows the payment provider's account configuration
tradeoffs: Prevents the display-versus-charged currency mismatch that erodes trust at checkout; needs explicit handling wherever the two diverge
